slug: 图灵破了密码赢了二战然后被他自己的国家化学阉割
title: 图灵破了密码赢了二战然后被他自己的国家化学阉割

outline.concept: Institutions don't store gratitude — they process by category, not contribution. Legal systems see 'homosexual offender,' not 'war hero who happens to be homosexual.' There is no mechanism where past service overrides present classification. Turing's Enigma work was simply in a different bureaucratic drawer than his criminal file.
outline.query: 被遗忘 付出不被记住 功劳没用 恩将仇报 好人没好报

outline.concept: The blackmail cycle: criminalizing homosexuality created the very vulnerability (blackmail risk) that intelligence agencies then cited to classify gay people as security threats. The law manufactured the danger it claimed to defend against. Turing was both indispensable and, by definition, a liability — the definition being the law's own creation.
outline.query: 被抓住把柄 秘密被利用 弱点被控制 恶性循环 忠诚被质疑

outline.concept: Chemical castration as medicalized punishment — the state offered synthetic estrogen instead of prison and called it treatment. It didn't suppress desire; it caused gynecomastia, impotence, and cognitive fog. The body became the site of discipline. 'Therapy' was the word; violence was the practice. The choice between prison and chemical alteration isn't a choice.
outline.query: 强制治疗 身体被改变 失去控制 被迫吃药 副作用 没有选择权

outline.concept: The system worked as designed — this wasn't a malfunction. Norm-enforcing institutions apply rules categorically; that's their architecture. When the law destroys someone who saved millions, it reveals that the law's purpose was never justice or proportionality — it was maintaining a moral order, and it succeeded exactly as intended. The tragedy isn't the glitch; the tragedy is the feature.
outline.query: 制度冷漠 规则无情 体制问题 被规则伤害 合理的不公平
